prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens in a study or library setting. An older man with white hair and glasses stands in front of bookshelves filled with books. He wears a light-colored suit jacket, shirt, and tie, and holds an open dark-covered book in both hands, looking down at it as if reading. A “Classic Commercial Archives” watermark appears near the lower left.

The scene then cuts to a close-up of a cylindrical container filled with a pale, creamy substance. The top is open, and the surface of the contents is visible. Large overlaid text in the center reads “FAT,” while the container itself has the word “SHORTENING” printed on it. The image visually associates the contents with fat/shortening.

Next, the commercial cuts to a close-up product shot. A hand holds a red-and-yellow aerosol can of PAM in front of a softly blurred bookshelf background. The can has a red cap and a yellow upper label with the large brand name “PAM.” Smaller visible label elements include food imagery and text such as “Spray Calorie Food Dresser” and “100 Portions,” though some wording is partially unclear due to image quality.

In the final frame, the PAM can remains on the left, still held in a hand against the blurred bookshelf background. Large white block text appears to the right of the can in three stacked lines: “NO CHOLESTEROL,” “NO CALORIES,” and “NO FAT.” Very small fine print appears along the bottom, partially legible, including “Maximum added 1 gram fat, 7 calories,” though the full line is difficult to read. The “Classic Commercial Archives” watermark remains visible near the lower left, with a faint archive watermark across the lower area.
